North Carolina GLP-1 manufacturing site specifically named. Eclipses Lilly's LillyPod in total GPU count.

notes: Denmark's first AI supercomputer, #21 on TOP500. 191 NVIDIA DGX H100 systems with 1,528 H100 GPUs, 382 Intel Xeon Platinum CPUs, Quantum-2 InfiniBand. Peak 51.2 PFLOPS FP64, 1.51 exaflops FP16. Hosted at Digital Realty Copenhagen. Liquid cooling, 100% renewable energy. Built by Eviden (Atos). Funded ~DKK 700M by Novo Nordisk Foundation + EIFO. Novo Nordisk signed multiyear pharma research agreement. Supports NVIDIA BioNeMo protein design, genomics, drug discovery.

facility_name: NVIDIA Cambridge-1 Supercomputer -- Kao Data, Harlow
notes: UK's most powerful supercomputer dedicated to healthcare AI. 80 NVIDIA DGX A100 systems (640 A100 GPUs), ~400 PFLOPS AI compute, ~10 PFLOPS Linpack. $100M+ NVIDIA investment. Hosted at Kao Data Harlow with PUE 1.2, 100% free cooling, 100% renewable energy. Founding partners: AstraZeneca (MegaMolBART drug discovery), GSK, King's College London, Oxford Nanopore. Now integrated into NVIDIA DGX Cloud for broader EU/UK access. Green500 Top 5 at launch.

BioNTech's in-house near-exascale AI supercluster for mRNA drug discovery. 224 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 86,000 CPU cores, 1.7 PB persistent storage, 400 Gbps RoCE network. ~0.5 exaFLOPS FP16. Among world's top 100 most powerful clusters, top 20 H100 clusters. Hosted at Equinix Saint-Denis. Powered by renewable energy. Training 15B-parameter protein engineering models. BioNTech acquired InstaDeep for 562M GBP in 2023. AIchor orchestration platform. 15,000 experiments/month.

Pharma industry's first genomics-focused AI supercomputer in Iceland. NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD with 31 DGX H100 nodes totaling 248 H100 GPUs. Named 'Freyja'. deCODE curates 200+ PB de-identified human genomic data from ~3M individuals (incl 500K UK Biobank). Powers human diversity atlas, drug target discovery, biomarker identification, precision medicine models. NVIDIA BioNeMo and DGX Cloud integrated. Iceland location provides geothermal/renewable energy and natural cooling advantages.

facility_name: GSK AI Hub -- King's Cross, London (with Cerebras CS-1)
notes: GSK's dedicated AI drug discovery hub in London's King's Cross tech cluster. Features Cerebras CS-1 with 400,000 AI-optimized cores on a single wafer (WSE-1, 1.2T transistors, 18 GB on-chip memory). Trained EBERT genomics model in 2.5 days vs 24 days on 16-node GPU cluster. Also uses NVIDIA DGX A100 infrastructure and Cambridge-1 access. Located near Francis Crick Institute and Alan Turing Institute. 10M GBP investment. Genetic/genomic data for drug target discovery.

facility_name: Wellcome Sanger Institute Data Centre -- Hinxton Campus
notes: One of Europe's largest biosciences data centres. Morgan Building houses four 250m2 halls, 400 racks, 35,000+ cores (growing with GPUs). 4MW facility. PUE 1.6-1.8 (targeting 1.4-1.6). CCHP plant captures waste heat for building heating. Produces 10 billion bases raw sequence data per day. Schneider Electric EcoStruxure IT management with 300+ custom APC PDUs. Computational output increased 50% while reducing energy 33%. Growing GPU estate for AI/ML genomics workloads.

company: European Molecular Biology Laboratory - European Bioinformatics Institute
notes: World's largest open biological data repository. 390+ PB raw biological data. 32,000+ CPU cores, 84 TB RAM, NVIDIA Ampere GPUs (~680 TFLOPS). Hosts European Genome-phenome Archive (123 PB cumulative), UniProt, Ensembl, and other critical databases. 24/7/365 web services. Second main IT centre at EMBL Heidelberg, Germany. Solar panels on South Building reduce energy 25%. Data volumes growing exponentially -- strong driver for cooling upgrades.

facility_name: AstraZeneca Discovery Centre (DISC) Energy Centre -- Cambridge Biomedical Campus
notes: AstraZeneca's global R&D HQ. 70,000m2 facility with 19,000m2 labs on Cambridge Biomedical Campus. Dedicated Energy Centre with secure data centre for scientific computing. Founding partner of NVIDIA Cambridge-1 (MegaMolBART drug discovery model). Also collaborates with IonQ/AWS/NVIDIA on quantum-accelerated hybrid workflows. AI-driven robotics, high-throughput screening, digital pathology. RIBA East Award 2025. Hub for 2,200+ research scientists.

facility_name: Volkswagen Group HPC DC -- Green Mountain DC2-Telemark, Rjukan, Norway (2.75MW)
notes: Volkswagen Group operates 2.75MW of HPC capacity in 2 dedicated data halls at Green Mountain's DC2-Telemark facility in Rjukan, Norway. Used by VW Passenger Cars and Audi for crash test simulations and virtual wind tunnel tests. Climate-neutral operation using 100% Norwegian hydroelectric power. Saves ~5,800 tons CO2/year vs fossil-powered DC. Built in 6 months using Schneider Electric prefabricated modular infrastructure. Indirect free cooling exploits Norway's cold/wet climate. Green Mountain PUE typically 1.10-1.20. NOTE: This is free-air/water cooling, NOT liquid-cooled server hardware -- included as automotive sector DC with advanced cooling.

facility_name: BMW D3 Platform Data Center -- Unterschleissheim, Munich (Autonomous Driving HPC)
notes: BMW Group's D3 (Data-Driven Development) platform data center is sited near the Autonomous Driving Campus in Unterschleissheim, Munich. Houses 230+ petabytes of storage, 100,000+ cores, and 200+ GPUs. Collects 1,500+ TB raw driving data daily, pushes 50PB to Hardware-in-the-Loop stations every 2 weeks via 96x100Gbps connection (3.75 Tbit/s). Operated by DXC Technology. Cooling type not publicly disclosed -- but at this scale and density, liquid cooling is highly probable. NOTE: Cooling technology not confirmed; included as major automotive sector AI/HPC DC.

facility_name: Stellium DC / Submer / ExxonMobil OCP Immersion Cooling Deployment -- Newcastle, UK
address: Newcastle upon Tyne, Tyne and Wear
notes: Stellium Data Centres deployed Submer immersion cooling technology at their Newcastle campus using ExxonMobil's Submer-validated DC 3235 Super coolant. Compute powered by MiTAC Capri 2 servers with AMD CPUs (Circle B supplied). This is the first OCP ORv3 (Open Rack v3) showcase deployed in Europe and reportedly the largest OCP stack in Europe. Proof of concept completed January 2025. Open day held February 12, 2025 for customers and OCP representatives. MW capacity not publicly disclosed.

facility_name: Hyundai Motor Group / NVIDIA Blackwell AI Factory -- South Korea (50,000 GPUs, Planned)
notes: Hyundai Motor Group announced an NVIDIA Blackwell-powered AI Factory deploying 50,000 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs for integrated AI model training, validation, and deployment. Supports in-vehicle AI, autonomous driving, smart factory automation, robotics, LLM development, and digital twin simulation. Part of NVIDIA's $3 billion investment in Korea's physical AI ecosystem. Exact location, MW, and cooling technology not disclosed. At 50,000 Blackwell GPUs, liquid cooling is virtually certain (NVIDIA Blackwell B200 requires liquid cooling at scale). NOTE: Cooling type not confirmed; included as major automotive sector AI compute build.

company: University of Bristol / Bristol Centre for Supercomputing (BriCS)
facility_name: Isambard-AI Phase 2 -- UK National AI Research Resource (AIRR)
notes: UK sovereign AI supercomputer launched July 2025 at National Composites Centre in Bristol. 5,448 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips via HPE Cray EX. 23 exaflops AI performance. 100% fan-less direct liquid cooling with PUE 1.1. All 5MW from zero-carbon grid supply. Part of UK AI Research Resource (AIRR) with 20x expansion planned by 2030. 225M GBP Phase 2 investment. Government-backed sovereign compute for UK AI research and public sector.

UK's 750M GBP national supercomputer planned for Edinburgh. 31MW heat output projected. Housed in EPCC Advanced Computing Facility new wing (31M GBP). Heat recovery via old mine workings studied. No longer called exascale. Target early 2027 operational. Major sovereign compute investment for AI, science, industry. Liquid cooling virtually certain but specs not yet finalized.

EuroHPC AI Factory at HLRS Stuttgart. HPE-built liquid-cooled NVIDIA GB200 NVL4 (Blackwell) system. 15+ ExaFLOPS peak AI inference performance. 55M EUR budget (50% EuroHPC, 50% German Federal/State). Delivery Q2 2026. Operated under EU data security regulations with free access for eligible European users. AI Factory opened April 2025 on existing infra; Blackwell upgrade arriving mid-2026.

company: GRNET (National Infrastructures for Research and Technology)
facility_name: DAEDALUS Supercomputer + Pharos AI Factory -- GRNET Lavrio
notes: EuroHPC mid-range supercomputer in Greece. HPE NVIDIA GH200 DLC architecture. 89+ petaflops. Paired with Pharos AI Factory for European AI innovation. Renewable energy powered with advanced cooling. Installation summer 2026, full services by fall 2026. Supports AI, medicine, meteorology, big data, smart transport.

company: AIST (National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology)
facility_name: ABCI 3.0 -- AIST Kashiwa (Japan National AI Compute)
notes: Japan's sovereign AI supercomputer. 6,128 NVIDIA H200 GPUs on HPE Cray XD. 6.22 ExaFLOPS half precision. 3.0 ExaFLOPS single precision. 7-13x faster than ABCI 2.0. Direct liquid cooling + hot water/air hybrid (70kW/rack). 6MW capacity. METI-funded via Economic Security Fund as part of 1B USD sovereign AI initiative. Operational since Jan 2025.

facility_name: South Korea National AI Computing Center -- Samsung SDS Consortium, Haenam
notes: South Korea's flagship sovereign AI compute project. Samsung SDS consortium (Naver Cloud, KT, Kakao, Samsung Electronics, Samsung C&T). 2.5T KRW (1.7B USD) contract. 51% public / 49% private SPC structure. 15,000 GPUs by 2028, expanding to 50,000 by 2030. SoLaSeaDo Data Center Park in Haenam. Part of Korea's 50-DC / 2GW sovereign AI plan. Liquid cooling highly probable but specs not yet disclosed.

facility_name: NCHC Nano4 -- Taiwan National AI Supercomputer (ASUS DLC)
notes: Taiwan's sovereign AI supercomputer. NCHC Nano4 with ASUS Direct-to-Chip liquid cooling. PUE 1.18. NVIDIA HGX H200 cluster at 81.55 PFLOPS (TOP500 #29). GB200 NVL72 Blackwell phase deploying in 2026. Part of Taiwan's 'Ten Major AI Infrastructure Projects.' ASUS AIDC automated setup (3 weeks to 3 days). Highest-density AI facility in Taiwan. NVIDIA + Foxconn AI factory for TSMC also planned.

facility_name: Yotta Shakti Cloud -- NVIDIA Blackwell Supercluster, Greater Noida
notes: India's largest sovereign AI deployment. 20,736 liquid-cooled NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra (B300) GPUs. 2B USD investment. 60MW D2 DC at Greater Noida (scalable to 250MW), plus Navi Mumbai campus (scalable to 2GW). 10,000+ GPUs committed to IndiaAI Mission for sovereign foundation model development. NVIDIA DGX Cloud cluster (1B USD 4-year engagement). Shakti Studio AI platform with NVIDIA NIM. Expected operational Aug 2026.

company: CSIRO (Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation)
facility_name: CSIRO Virga -- Australia's First Sovereign AI Supercomputer, Canberra
notes: Australia's first sovereign AI supercomputer with liquid cooling. 111 Dell PowerEdge XE9640 servers with 448 NVIDIA H100 GPUs. 14.94 PFLOPS (TOP500 #72). Hybrid DLC. 14.5M AUD investment. Housed at CDC Hume DC in Canberra. First Dell AI Factory liquid-cooled deployment in Australia. Supports CSIRO machine learning and AI for industry/economy growth.

facility_name: Macquarie IC3 Super West -- Sovereign AI Factory, Sydney
notes: Australian sovereign AI factory. Macquarie + Dell Technologies bringing NVIDIA-powered sovereign AI factories to Australia. 47MW facility with DLC capability. Fungible halls for air/liquid/hybrid cooling. Phase I 350M AUD with 6MW initial IT fit-out. Part of 65MW Macquarie Park campus. Opening Sep 2026. Purpose-built for high-density AI compute.

facility_name: SDAIA Hexagon Data Center -- Riyadh (World's Largest Government DC)
notes: World's largest government data center. 480MW capacity. 30M sqft. Tier IV certified (Uptime Institute). TIA-942 compliant. LEED Gold. Direct liquid cooling + smart cooling + hybrid systems for Saudi desert climate. Renewable energy integration. Part of SDAIA strategy and Vision 2030. Foundation stone laid Jan 2026.

facility_name: NEOM DataVolt AI Factory -- Oxagon (Net-Zero AI Campus)
notes: 1.5GW net-zero AI factory at NEOM Oxagon. 5B USD Phase 1 (300MW operational by 2028). Red Sea seawater cooling eliminates fresh water consumption. LG Electronics advanced cooling partnership. 100% renewable energy powered. Located on Red Sea coast in NEOM's industrial/innovation zone. Multiple computing density architectures. Region's first net-zero AI factory claim.

facility_name: Stargate UAE -- G42 AI Infrastructure Cluster, Abu Dhabi
notes: Largest AI infrastructure investment outside US. 1GW Stargate compute cluster (Phase 1: 200MW live 2026). Part of 5GW UAE-US AI Campus (10 sq miles). Built by G42/Khazna, operated by OpenAI and Oracle. NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GB300 systems. Cisco zero-trust security. Nuclear + solar + gas power. Sovereign AI for UAE and global inferencing.

facility_name: Stargate Norway -- Nscale/Aker/OpenAI AI Gigafactory, Narvik
notes: OpenAI's first European data center. 100,000 NVIDIA GPUs by end 2026. 230MW initial capacity (expandable to 520MW). Closed-loop D2C liquid cooling. 100% renewable hydropower. Surplus GPU heat reused for local enterprises. Kvandal near Narvik in Northern Norway. Part of OpenAI for Countries programme. Nscale + Aker partnership.

facility_name: YTL Green Data Center Park -- Malaysia Sovereign AI (NVIDIA GB200 DLC)
notes: Malaysia's sovereign AI data center. NVIDIA liquid-cooled GB200 NVL72 GPUs operational. 600MW campus on 1,640 acres in Johor. 500MW solar plant for renewable power. 2.38B USD invested (half DC infra, half AI solutions). Malaysia's first LLM (Ilmu). PM Anwar Ibrahim + Jensen Huang announced at APEC 2025. Global hyperscalers as customers. 5.9B MYR government AI budget 2026.

company: BDx Indonesia (Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison + Lintasarta + BDx)
facility_name: BDx CGK4 AI Campus -- Indonesia's First Sovereign AI Data Center
notes: Indonesia's first sovereign AI data center. BDx consortium (Indosat, Lintasarta, BDx). 500MW total campus at Jatiluhur. Direct-to-chip liquid cooling at scale (first in Indonesia). 120kW/rack density. NVIDIA DGX-Ready certified. NVIDIA accelerated computing platform. Renewable energy powered. Part of Indonesia's Golden Vision 2045 sovereign AI strategy. Indosat first in SEA to adopt NVIDIA GB200 NVL72.

facility_name: NSCC ASPIRE 2A -- Singapore National Supercomputer (Warm Water DLC)
notes: Singapore's national supercomputer with pioneering tropical warm-water DLC. ASPIRE 2A: 10 PFLOPS, Cray DLC with CDUs, runs at 33.5C+ with no AC (first tropical DLC deployment). Next-gen 100+ PFLOPS system planned for H1 2026. Part of Singapore's sovereign compute infrastructure alongside IMDA DC-CFA2 200MW capacity call (PUE < 1.30 required).

facility_name: Eclairion Sovereign AI Data Center -- Bruyeres-le-Chatel (Mistral AI)
notes: French sovereign AI data center for Mistral AI. 40MW DLC-throughout facility 40km south of Paris. 18,000 NVIDIA GB200 GPUs via Scaleway. 66 containers at 600kW each, 20 racks per container. Eclairion raised 50M EUR (Feb 2025). First DC operational 2027. Part of Macron's 109B EUR AI investment strategy. Renewable energy via Enedis substations. NOTE: FluidStack withdrew from France in March 2026, pivoting to US.

facility_name: NVIDIA Sovereign AI Lab -- Mevo Carmel, Israel
notes: NVIDIA's own sovereign AI lab in Israel. 10,000 sqm facility at Mevo Carmel. 30MW. Hundreds of liquid-cooled Blackwell + Grace Blackwell Superchip systems. Full-stack networking (Spectrum-X800, Quantum-X800, BlueField-3). Part of NVIDIA's massive Israel investment. Jensen Huang described implications of building AI infrastructure in Israel as 'profound.'

facility_name: Telenor AI Factory -- Norway's First Sovereign AI Cloud (Skygard)
notes: Norway's first sovereign AI cloud platform. Telenor + Red Hat (MWC 2026 launch). Supermicro GPU infrastructure. NVIDIA-accelerated. All data stays within Norway. Skygard DCs in Oslo. Complements Stargate Norway (Narvik). Part of broader Norwegian sovereign AI strategy.

facility_name: Meta (Facebook) Odense Data Center -- 45MW Waste Heat to 7,000 Homes via Fjernvarme Fyn
notes: Meta's Odense data center campus (50,000 sqm) recovers waste heat via a water-based system. District heating water circulates through insulated steel pipes into rooftop copper coils across 176 cooling units, picking up low-temperature heat, then returns to Fjernvarme Fyn's 45MW heat pump plant -- Denmark's largest at commissioning. Delivers approximately 125,000 MWh of district heating annually to nearly 7,000 households in Odense. Ramboll is engineering partner. Supports Fjernvarme Fyn's 100% renewable energy goal by 2030.

facility_name: Microsoft / Fortum -- Espoo & Kirkkonummi Data Centers (World's Largest DC Heat Recovery, 40% District Heat)
notes: Described as the world's largest data center heat recovery project and Finland's largest single ICT investment. Microsoft's two new data centers in Espoo and Kirkkonummi will provide approximately 40% of all district heat needed for 250,000 customers. Fortum invests EUR 225 million in heat pump plants. 75% of waste heat used annually. Saves 400,000 tonnes CO2/year at full capacity. Heat pump plant construction started Jan 2025, noisy construction ending June 2026. AFRY is technical consultant.

facility_name: AWS Tallaght Data Center -- Ireland's First District Heating Scheme (100% DC Heat)
notes: Ireland's first large-scale district heating network. AWS provides 100% of heat to the Tallaght District Heating Scheme free of charge. Heats 47,000 sqm of public buildings (County Hall, library, Innovation Centre), 3,000 sqm commercial space, and 135 affordable rental apartments. Heatworks is Ireland's first not-for-profit energy utility (formed 2020 by South Dublin County Council). 704 metric tons CO2 abated in 2024. 60% carbon reduction vs conventional heating.

facility_name: Equinix HE3/HE5 Helsinki -- World's First DC District Heating (Since 2010, with Helen)
notes: Pioneered the world's first data center district heating project in 2010 at Kanavaranta, Helsinki. Partnership with Helen (Helsinki's energy company) expanded to HE3 (Suvilahti) and HE5 (Viikinmaki). Heat pumps raise DC waste heat temperature for transfer into Helen's 1,409km district heating network. Heats up to ~1,500 one-bedroom apartments per site. Extended again in Oct 2024 for even more homes. Over a decade of continuous operation.

facility_name: STACK Infrastructure OSL01A Oslo -- 3.5MW Heat Export to 5,000 Homes (Hafslund Oslo Celsio)
notes: STACK's OSL01A data center near the heart of Oslo exports 3.5MW of heat energy into Oslo's district heating system, representing ~25GWh of energy per year. Heats up to 5,000 homes. Partnership with Hafslund Oslo Celsio, which operates a 60-mile thermal distribution system. Concept conceived 2018, tested and proven, dedicated heat-exchange plant built on campus with retrofitted insulated pipework and cooling coils. Fully operational Sept 2022. Frees up Celsio's green electricity for transport electrification.

facility_name: DigiPlex Stockholm -- 10,000 Apartments Heated via Stockholm Exergi (World's First Retrofit)
notes: World's first retrofit of an already-operational data center with an indirect evaporative air-to-air cooling solution for heat recovery into district heating. Partnership with Stockholm Exergi heats up to 10,000 modern residential apartments. 90% of Stockholm buildings connected to district heating. City's goal: 10% of heating needs from DC waste heat. Won 2018 Energy Awards Renewable Energy Technology of the Year in London.

facility_name: DigiPlex Ulven Oslo -- 5,000 Apartments via Fortum Oslo Varme
notes: DigiPlex campus in Ulven, Oslo recovers waste heat to warm up to 5,000 apartments via partnership with Fortum Oslo Varme. Fortum provides cold water cooling and recovers waste heat through heat pumps on campus. 100% renewable electricity. Operational from autumn 2019.

facility_name: Telia Espoo Data Center -- 25,000 Apartments via Fortum District Heating
notes: Telia's Espoo data center (built summer 2019) transfers waste heat directly to Fortum's district heating network, providing heat for 25,000 apartments in the city of Espoo. This is one of the largest single-facility DC heat recovery outputs in Finland.

facility_name: Yandex Mantsala Data Center -- 75% of City Homes Heated, CO2 Cut 50%
notes: Yandex data center in Mantsala, Finland pipes hot exhaust air from servers into the town's district heating system. Heat pumps raise water temperature from 30-45C to 55-60C for district heating. Supplies up to 75% of local homes (town of ~20,000 residents). Cut utility gas consumption by 50% and CO2 emissions by 40-50%. Reduces heating costs for residents by 5%.

facility_name: Apple Foulum/Viborg Data Center -- District Heating via Viborg Varme
notes: Apple's Foulum Data Center near Viborg, Denmark. EUR 850M total investment, 1.8M sqft planned. Connected to Viborg Varme district heating via EUR 50M heat pipe investment. Currently operating at ~7MW capacity. Powered by world's largest onshore wind turbines. Adjacent to Tjele grid hub with 1.5GW HVDC line to Norwegian hydropower. Surplus renewable energy fed back to Danish grid. Heat recovery infrastructure built 2022-2023.

facility_name: Bahnhof Pionen White Mountains -- Underground Bunker DC with Stockholm District Heating
notes: Famous 'James Bond Villain' data center in a former Cold War nuclear bunker, 30 meters under granite in Vita Berg Park, Stockholm. Two heat pumps installed routing waste server air through a 70-meter pipe to the district heating network's main pipeline. Saves 275 MWh/year and EUR 40,000 in annual electricity costs. Features waterfalls, greenhouse-style NOC, glass conference room.

facility_name: atNorth DEN01 Ballerup -- 22.5MW DLC with Heat Recovery to 8,000+ Homes (Vestforbraending)
notes: atNorth's DEN01 is a 22.5MW metro colocation site in Ballerup, Greater Copenhagen. Uses direct liquid cooling (DLC) with PUE under 1.2. Partnership with Vestforbraending (Denmark's largest waste-to-energy company) to use warm water byproduct from DLC to heat 8,000+ homes via district heating network from 2028. Operational Q1 2026.

facility_name: atNorth SWE01 Stockholm -- 12MW with Stockholm Exergi Heat Recovery (20,000 Apartments)
notes: atNorth's SWE01 campus in Stockholm (12MW, 6,400 sqm, opened March 2022) recycles all residual heat through Stockholm Exergi's district heating plant. Could heat up to 20,000 apartments. SWE02 (30MW, scheduled Q4 2027) will expand this further.

facility_name: atNorth FIN04 Kouvola -- 430MW Mega Site with KSS Energia Heat Recovery
notes: atNorth's fourth Finland campus (FIN04) in Kouvola, 139km NE of Helsinki. Massive 430MW capacity with path to several hundred additional MW. First phase 60MW ready H2 2025. All new atNorth facilities are liquid cooling ready. Excess heat recycled through KSS Energia heating plant to provide heating and hot water for local community.

facility_name: atNorth FIN02 Espoo -- Kesko Retail Store Heat Reuse Partnership (Live March 2026)
notes: atNorth FIN02 data center in Espoo exports waste heat to adjacent Kesko retail store (Finland's largest retailer). Partnership went live November 2025. Supplies almost all heating for the store. Saves ~200 tonnes CO2 equivalent per year. Reduces Kesko's district heating emissions by 0.9% from this single store. Supports Kesko's 58.8% Scope 1+2 reduction target.

facility_name: Green Mountain DC1-Stavanger (Rennesoy) -- Fjord-Cooled with Lobster Farm + Fish Heat Reuse
notes: Green Mountain DC1-Stavanger on Rennesoy island is built inside concrete buildings within mountain caves. Gravity-fed fjord seawater cooling at 8C flows through the data center. Warmed to exactly 20C -- the ideal temperature for lobster breeding. Adjacent Hima Seafood facility uses the waste heat for world's first land-based lobster farm. Also supports fish production with stable water temperatures, reducing additional electricity consumption.

facility_name: Green Mountain RJU1-Rjukan -- Waste Heat to Land-Based Trout Farm (1.75MW Phase 1)
notes: Green Mountain's RJU1 facility in Rjukan uses excess heat to warm water for a land-based trout farm approximately 800 meters from the data center. Phase 1 operational at 1.75MW capacity. Plans to scale to 8MW in Phase 2. Part of Green Mountain's broader heat reuse strategy across multiple Norwegian sites.

facility_name: White Data Center Bibai -- Snow-Cooled DC with Eel Farm + Greenhouse (Hokkaido, Japan)
notes: White Data Center in Bibai, Hokkaido uses a unique snow cooling system: snow collected in winter is stored in an insulated mound. Server heat slowly melts the snow, and the cold meltwater cools antifreeze pipes. After cooling the DC, water exits at 33C -- ideal for Japanese eel farming. 1,700 elvers imported Jan 2024, first Yuki Unagi (snow eel) shipment July 2024. Adjacent greenhouse grows mushrooms, coffee beans, abalones, sea urchins, and spinach using waste heat. World-unique circular model.

facility_name: CloudHQ CDG Campus Corbeil-Essonnes -- 140MW with Heat Recovery to 7,000 Homes (Paris Region)
notes: CloudHQ's CDG Campus in the Paris region (Leonardo da Vinci Business Park, Essonne) is described as the largest individual data center in France. Phase 1: 140MW, two buildings totaling 53,000 sqm with 24 data halls each. Inaugurated June 2025. Heat recovery system connected to Corbeil-Essonnes district heating network, warming up to 7,000 homes -- a first in Ile-de-France. Full campus operational by 2027.

facility_name: Digital Realty (Interxion) Digital Park Fechenheim Frankfurt -- 200MW with Mainova District Heating (3,600 Homes)
notes: Digital Realty's Digital Park Fechenheim in Frankfurt will host 11 data centers across 100,000 sqm (1M sqft) with 200MW capacity when fully built out by ~2028. Mainova AG partnership for 20MW waste heat feed into city-wide district heating network. Industrial heat pumps to be installed in a listed boiler house on the Digital Park campus. Will heat approximately 3,600 households. First Frankfurt DC to feed waste heat into city network.

facility_name: Hemiko / OPDC Old Oak Park Royal -- UK's First DC Heat Network (9,000+ Homes, London)
notes: UK's first data center waste heat network. Hemiko named delivery partner by Old Oak and Park Royal Development Corporation (OPDC). Five phases from 2028 to 2040 delivering 95GWh/year of low-carbon heat. 9,000+ homes, businesses, and Central Middlesex Hospital. Ambient loop network collects low-temperature heat from DCs, Tube tunnels, etc. Building-level heat pumps boost temperature. GBP 63M Phase 1, GBP 600M total. 264% efficiency (vs 80-90% for gas boilers). GBP 36M government funding.

facility_name: Tencent Tianjin Binhai Data Center -- Magnetic Suspension Heat Pump for Residential Heating
notes: Tencent's Tianjin Binhai Data Center uses a magnetic suspension heat pump for waste heat recovery. Only 1/10th of generated waste heat satisfies heating needs of all office buildings; remainder exported to residential district heating. First LEED V4 O+M Platinum certification in China. MIIT National Green Data Center certification. Uses natural cooling and waste heat recovery. Economic response verified the feasibility of DC-to-district-heating via heat pumps in China.

facility_name: Penta Infra AMS01 Haarlem -- Waste Heat to 1,100 Businesses (Polderwarmte)
notes: Penta Infra's AMS01 in Haarlem (acquired from KPN) is being developed to 8MW IT capacity. Letter of intent with Polderwarmte to redirect 100% of residual heat into district heating system for Waarderpolder business park (1,100 businesses). Ready H1 2026. Municipality of Haarlem supports the project toward gas-free Waarderpolder by 2040.

facility_name: Leafcloud Distributed Leaf Sites -- Amsterdam Cloud Servers Heating Apartments, Pools, Nursing Homes
notes: Leafcloud operates distributed micro data centers ('Leaf sites') installed inside existing buildings across Amsterdam. Each site uses advanced liquid cooling capturing 95%+ of server heat for direct building use. Targets: swimming pools, nursing homes, apartment buildings, hospitals, office complexes, ATES systems. Reduces CO2 by up to 1,776 kg per kW-year. Reduces energy costs by up to 40% for public pools. Joined NVIDIA AI startup programme. Cloud services: Kubernetes, GPU, OpenStack.

facility_name: Highlander Hainan Underwater Intelligent Computing Center
notes: World's first commercially operational underwater data center cluster. Two 1,300-tonne capsules submerged 35m underwater off Hainan Island use the South China Sea as a passive cooling system. Each capsule holds 24 server racks (up to 500 servers). Combined cluster has ~1,200 servers capable of handling 7,000 DeepSeek conversations per second. PUE 1.1 -- 30% better than China's national average of 1.5. Expansion to 100 capsules across 68,000 sqm of seabed planned within 5 years. Saves 90% of cooling energy vs. traditional DCs.

facility_name: HiCloud Shanghai Lingang Wind-Powered Underwater Data Center
notes: World's first offshore wind-powered underwater data center. Phase 1 is a 2.3MW demonstration facility (198 server racks) designated by China's NDRC as a national model for green, low-carbon innovation. Phase 2 scales to 24MW with PUE below 1.15. Over 90% power from offshore wind farms. Initial investment of 1.6 billion yuan ($222.8M). Reduces overall energy use by 30-40% vs traditional DCs. Long-term vision: 500MW subsea deployment.

facility_name: Microsoft Project Natick -- Northern Isles (Decommissioned Research)
notes: Pioneering underwater data center research project. Phase 2 capsule (12m long, 2m diameter) held 864 servers across 12 racks at 20kW/rack. Submerged at 117 feet in Orkney for 2 years. Key finding: 1/8th the server failure rate of land-based equivalents. Nitrogen atmosphere and absence of human activity identified as primary factors. Project officially ended June 2024 -- underwater pods cannot be easily upgraded for AI GPU workloads. Lessons now applied to Microsoft's land-based liquid immersion cooling strategy.

facility_name: Aikido Technologies Offshore Wind-Turbine Data Center (Prototype)
notes: San Francisco startup building data centers inside offshore wind turbine platforms. Prototype: 100kW unit with refurbished Vestas V-17 turbine launching in Norway North Sea by end of 2026. Full-scale concept (AO60DC): semi-submersible platform with 3 ballast-filled legs, each housing a 3-4MW liquid-cooled data hall (total 9-12MW per turbine). Freshwater ballast cooling with heat dissipated to sea through steel walls. Oil-and-gas style semi-submersible design. First commercial project targeted for UK in 2028.

facility_name: Lefdal Mine Datacenter (Underground Olivine Mine, Fjord-Cooled)
notes: Former olivine mine 60m below sea level and 700m inside a mountain on Norway's west coast. 12 modular heat exchangers (90MW total cooling) draw 8C water from 100m depth in a 500m-deep fjord. Supports up to 300kW+ per rack via DLC and immersion cooling (designing 400kW solutions). 100% hydro and wind power. Guaranteed PUE 1.15. Near-zero WUE. Hosts Olivia -- Norway's most powerful supercomputer (HPE Cray EX, 13.2 petaflops, 64,512 CPUs + 304 GPUs, ranked #22 Green500). 225M NOK ($22.6M) system inaugurated June 2025. Expandable to 200MW and 120,000 sqm whitespace.

facility_name: Stargate Norway -- Nscale/Aker/OpenAI Arctic AI Gigafactory
notes: OpenAI's first European AI data center (Stargate Norway). Located in Kvandal near Narvik in the Arctic Circle. 230MW initial capacity powered entirely by renewable hydropower. Targeting 100,000 Nvidia GPUs by end of 2026. Closed-loop direct-to-chip liquid cooling. Waste heat recycled for local industry. ~$1B Phase 1 investment ($250M+ equity each from Nscale and Aker). Expansion to additional 290MW planned. Arctic location provides cheap power (below European average) and natural cooling. OpenAI's first AI gigafactory under 'For Countries' programme.

facility_name: Verne Keflavik Campus -- Nscale Blackwell Ultra Deployment (Iceland, Geothermal-Cooled)
notes: Verne's 40-acre Keflavik campus (140MW+ IT capacity) in Iceland hosts one of the Nordic region's largest liquid-cooled GPU installations. Nscale signed a 15MW lease (Nov 2025) to deploy 4,600 Nvidia Blackwell Ultra GPUs across 2026 in an 85% liquid-cooled / 15% air-cooled configuration. 100% renewable energy from geothermal and hydropower. Iceland's subarctic climate provides natural free cooling. Setting a new standard for sustainable high-performance computing.

facility_name: NEOM Oxagon DataVolt AI Data Center Campus (Desert, Red Sea-Cooled)
notes: Landmark $5B AI data center campus at NEOM's Oxagon industrial zone on the Red Sea coast. 1.5GW total capacity (Phase 1: 300MW by 2028). Net-zero design powered entirely by renewable energy. Red Sea seawater cooling eliminates freshwater consumption in the desert. LG/Shaker providing sandstorm-resistant cooling equipment. Strategic location near subsea fiber-optic cables, green hydrogen projects, and offshore wind. Part of Saudi Arabia's massive pivot from 'The Line' to AI infrastructure.

facility_name: XDS Datacentres / ICS Arabia -- Immersion-Cooled Desert Data Centers (Riyadh & Jeddah)
notes: Saudi Arabia's first purpose-built immersion-cooled data centers. 10MW capacity across Riyadh and Jeddah facilities. Full immersion cooling supporting 368kW per rack -- critical for operating in extreme desert temperatures. 15-year agreement signed July 2025. Desert Dragons facility handover to XDS by June 2026. Targeting Tier III certification. Redundant N+N power and cooling. Designed for AI, GPUaaS, cloud-native compute, and hyperscale edge workloads.

facility_name: Bahnhof Bunkerberget -- Underground WWII Bunker Data Center (Gothenburg)
notes: New underground data center in a former WWII bomb shelter / Cold War SKF ball-bearing factory bunker in Gothenburg. 6,000 sqm mountain room with 9m ceiling height (28,000 sqm total site). 12MW capacity for high-density workloads. Liquid cooling zones + redundant free cooling. Heat reuse systems planned. 100% renewable power. Tier III+ equivalent. Follows Bahnhof's Pionen model of converting military infrastructure to DC use.

facility_name: CyberFort The Bunker -- Former Nuclear Bunker Data Center (UK)
notes: Former military-grade nuclear bunker 30m underground with 10-tonne blast doors, converted to a high-security data center. Closed-loop cooling consuming minimal fresh water. Underground thermal inertia provides natural cooling advantage. Two UK sites: Ash (Kent) and Newbury. Serves government, defense, and financial services clients. Renewable energy sourcing. Physical security: military-grade nuclear bunker design.

facility_name: University of Munster CIT -- ZutaCore HyperCool Waterless DLC
notes: University HPC data center retrofitted with ZutaCore HyperCool waterless two-phase DLC. Eliminated water leakage risk, reduced energy costs significantly. Plans to expand HyperCool to all future HPC needs. Small academic DC, ideal Solar Steam prospect.

facility_name: Adacen / DUG Nomad -- Silver Spring Immersion Cooling Demo
notes: Adacen's 12401 Prosperity Drive facility hosts DUG Nomad prototype immersion cooling container. 214,000 sq ft facility with 6x 2.4MW generators. DUG Nomad units available in 10/20/40-foot containers. Demonstration site for customer evaluations.

facility_name: Asperitas / UNICOM Engineering -- DFCX Immersion Lab (Plano, TX)
notes: Asperitas DFCX immersion cooling platform deployed in UNICOM's Plano TX lab to validate immersion-ready server configurations under production conditions. Single-contract path to immersion-cooled infrastructure. Edge, cloud, and HPC support.

facility_name: Colovore SJC02 -- Liquid-Cooled AI Colocation (Santa Clara)
notes: Pioneer liquid-cooled colocation in Silicon Valley. SJC02 is 9MW, supports 17kW-500kW+ per rack via DLC and RDHx. NVIDIA DGX Ready certified. Customers include Cerebras, Lambda Cloud, Cirrascale. Acquired by King Street for rapid scaling.

facility_name: Osaka University Cybermedia Center -- OCTOPUS (Asetek DLC)
notes: 1.463 PFLOPS hybrid HPC system with Asetek hot water DLC on 236 Skylake nodes + 44 KNL nodes + 37 P100 GPU nodes. RackCDU captures 60-80% of server heat, cutting cooling costs 50%. University campus DC under 5MW. Established liquid cooling reference.

facility_name: NERSC Cech Early Access System -- Rittal DLC (Berkeley Lab)
notes: Early access system for NERSC-10 Doudna supercomputer. Dell IR7000 direct liquid-cooled rack + first US deployment of Rittal V3.5 CDU. NVIDIA Vera-Rubin platform pending. Cech delivered Jan 2026, full Doudna late 2026. National lab small-scale HPC.

facility_name: Vertiv CoolCenter Immersion -- Digital Realty ROM1 (Rome, Italy)
notes: Digital Realty's first Italian DC. 3MW+ initial capacity. Vertiv CoolCenter Immersion (launched Nov 2025) for AI/HPC. Free-cooling leveraging Rome climate. Carrier-neutral, optimized for AI/ML. Mediterranean connectivity hub. Expected live 2027.

facility_name: Nscale Glomfjord -- AI Data Center (Direct-to-Chip DLC)
notes: Nscale AI hyperscaler expanding existing 30MW facility to 60MW in Glomfjord, Norway. Arctic location. Closed-loop DLC drastically reducing water use. Initial 10MW phase target. Q2 2025 go-live. Renewable hydro/wind power. Sovereign European AI compute.

facility_name: Asperitas / STECON GROUP -- Southeast Asia Green DC (Planned)
notes: Asperitas and STECON GROUP partnership to accelerate Southeast Asia's green DC future. Immersion cooling critical in tropical climates where air cooling is least effective. Thailand/SEA region rapidly building AI infrastructure. High Solar Steam potential.

facility_name: CoolIT Starfield -- Manufacturing + Demo Facility (Calgary, Canada)
notes: CoolIT's Starfield facility in Calgary: manufacturing hub + Liquid Lab Innovation Center for customer demos and testing. 25X production scaling in 18 months. 2MW CDU launched April 2025. 300+ DC deployments worldwide. Acquired by Ecolab for $4.75B (March 2026).

facility_name: Iceotope Labs -- Precision Liquid Cooling Innovation Center (Sheffield, UK)
notes: Industry-first Precision Liquid Cooling lab at Iceotope HQ. Provides quantitative data for customer deployment decisions. Partnered with Efficiency IT, Juniper Networks, Engineered Fluids, Meta. KUL AI server: Gigabyte G293 + Intel Xeon. CRN Top 50 2026.

facility_name: nVent SCHROFF LFT -- Military Tactical Edge (VITA 48.4)
notes: nVent SCHROFF LFT module: VITA 48.4 standard, 300W/6U cooling, leak-proof quick-disconnect. Purpose-built for tactical edge: AI signal processing, radar, electronic warfare, HPC. SWaP-constrained defense systems. New Blaine MN factory early 2026.

facility_name: Submer MegaPod -- Modular DC Container (800kW per 40ft)
notes: Submer MegaPod: SmartPodXL + SmartPodXL+ technology, up to 800kW compute in 40ft modular footprint. Turnkey solution via modular DC partners. Fully operational in under 16 weeks. 400MW+ total liquid-cooled deployments globally. SmartPod EVO: 140kW per pod.

LiquidStack GigaModular: modular immersion container up to 1.5MW per unit. Quoting orders began mid-Q1 2026. DataTank technology proven at NTT (Japan) and Microsoft (Project Natick follow-on). Trane Technologies acquired LiquidStack Feb 2026. Pre-fab immersion at scale.
Google's most efficient data center campus globally. TTM PUE 1.04, quarterly PUE 1.04 (Q4 2025). Part of Central Ohio cluster. Too large for SOM but proves PUE 1.04 is achievable at scale.
Google Columbus campus. TTM PUE 1.06, quarterly PUE 1.05. Part of Central Ohio cluster with Lancaster and New Albany.
Google New Albany campus. TTM PUE 1.06, quarterly PUE 1.06. Third Ohio campus.
Google Eemshaven campus. TTM PUE 1.07, quarterly PUE 1.07. Netherlands site near Groningen.
Google Council Bluffs second campus. TTM PUE 1.08, quarterly PUE 1.07. Iowa wind energy.
Google Dublin campus. TTM PUE 1.08, quarterly PUE 1.08. Maritime climate advantage.
Google St. Ghislain campus. TTM PUE 1.08, quarterly PUE 1.07. Uses industrial canal water for cooling, no mechanical chillers.
Google Loudoun County campus 1. TTM PUE 1.08, quarterly PUE 1.07. Data Center Alley, Virginia.
Google Quilicura campus, Chile. TTM PUE 1.08, quarterly PUE 1.08. Only Google campus in South America. Desert climate advantage.
Google Berkeley County campus. TTM PUE 1.09, quarterly PUE 1.08.
Google Henderson campus. TTM PUE 1.09, quarterly PUE 1.08. Desert site near Las Vegas.
Google Papillion campus. TTM PUE 1.09, quarterly PUE 1.07.
Google Montgomery County campus. TTM PUE 1.09, quarterly PUE 1.06. Tennessee site.
Google Douglas County campus. TTM PUE 1.09, quarterly PUE 1.07.
Google Lenoir campus. TTM PUE 1.10, quarterly PUE 1.08.
Google Midlothian campus. TTM PUE 1.10, quarterly PUE 1.09. Texas site south of Dallas.
Google's first owned data center, The Dalles. TTM PUE 1.10, quarterly PUE 1.10. Oregon hydropower.
Google Jackson County campus. TTM PUE 1.10, quarterly PUE 1.09.
Google Inzai campus in Chiba Prefecture, Japan. TTM PUE 1.11, quarterly PUE 1.07. Serves Tokyo metro.
Google Mayes County campus. TTM PUE 1.12, quarterly PUE 1.09.
Google Changhua campus, Taiwan. TTM PUE 1.13, quarterly PUE 1.12. Tropical climate.
Google Singapore campus 1. TTM PUE 1.12, quarterly PUE 1.12. Tropical site, impressive efficiency given climate.
Google Storey County campus near Reno. TTM PUE 1.14, quarterly PUE 1.11.

facility_name: NREL Energy Systems Integration Facility (ESIF) HPC Data Center
notes: World-leading PUE of 1.036 (annualized average). Chiller-less design with warm-water direct liquid cooling captures 97% of waste heat for building heating. DCD Eco-Sustainability Award 2018. Under 2MW -- ideal SOM candidate for Solar Steam proof-of-concept.
IREN Canal Flats site in BC. 30MW, PUE 1.1 (air-cooled). BC cold climate advantage. Transitioning to liquid cooling for Blackwell GPUs.
IREN Mackenzie site. 80MW, PUE 1.1. BC hydropower, cold climate.
IREN Prince George site. 50MW, PUE 1.1. BC hydropower.
IREN Horizon 1 at Childress 750MW campus. 75MW Phase 1, liquid-cooled for Nvidia Blackwell GPUs. Projected PUE 1.05-1.10. Q4 2025 target delivery.

facility_name: Meta -- Global Fleet Average (15 owned campuses)
notes: Meta global fleet PUE 1.08 (2024). 15+ owned data centers. Does not publish per-site PUE. Fleet average is excellent but individual site data unavailable.
Baidu Yangquan Cloud Computing Center. Annual average PUE 1.09. Among China's most efficient DCs when first reported. 100,000+ servers.

KDDI containerized immersion-cooled micro DC. PUE 1.05-1.07. 12ft shipping container form factor. 43% power reduction for cooling. 94% reduction in temperature control electricity vs air cooling in trials. Under 1MW -- strong SOM candidate for edge deployments.

STACK/DigiPlex OSL03 Fetsund campus. PUE 1.1 via innovative Air-to-Air cooling exploiting Norway's climate. ~10MW. Now part of STACK Infrastructure portfolio. Potential SOM candidate at 10MW boundary.

facility_name: Chindata -- AI Data Center (Lingqiu campus, Datong)
notes: Chindata Lingqiu campus in Datong City. PUE 1.13 with hybrid cold-plate liquid + mag-lev phase-change air cooling. Part of AI Data Center Total Solution 2.0 (Oct 2024).

facility_name: Chindata -- AI DC Total Solution 2.0 (Immersive Reference Design)
notes: Chindata X-Cooling immersion liquid cooling solution achieves PUE as low as 1.10. Supports 8-150kW per cabinet. Oct 2024 announcement.
GDS Shanghai colocation complex. PUE 1.12 reported. One of China's largest third-party DC operators. Premium wholesale colocation.

Nebius Mantsala AI data center in Finland. PUE 1.1 (at high loads), 1.13 at full capacity. Free cooling, heat recovery heats 2,500 Finnish homes (20,000 MWh/yr). Expanding to 75MW. Custom servers saved 10 GWh in 2024.
Microsoft's most efficient data center region: Wyoming at PUE 1.11. Cold, dry climate. Microsoft global average FY24 was 1.16, FY25 was 1.17.
Microsoft Americas regional average PUE 1.16 (FY24 and FY25). Newest generation design PUE 1.12. Fleet-level data only, no per-site.

company: Vienna University of Technology / TU Wien
notes: Vienna Scientific Cluster 3, installed 2014, uses GRC immersion cooling with Supermicro servers. Mechanical PUE 1.02 (cooling overhead is only 2% of IT power). Under 1MW -- excellent SOM candidate for HPC/academic sector.
Telehouse North Two in London Docklands. Design PUE 1.16 using indirect adiabatic cooling -- first multi-floor urban DC to use this system. ~8MW. Potential SOM candidate.

OVHcloud hybrid immersion cooling achieves partial PUE (PPUE) of 1.004 -- nearly perfect efficiency for the cooling subsystem. 16 patents. Reduces server cooling power by up to 90%. IMPORTANT: This is PPUE (partial), not full facility PUE.

facility_name: Sabey Data Centers -- Columbia Campus (Quincy, WA)
notes: Sabey Columbia campus in Quincy, WA. Average PUE 1.2. Near hydropower dams, cool/dry climate. ~10MW. Potential SOM candidate at 10MW boundary.
Kao Data KLON-01 at Harlow campus. SLA-backed PUE 1.2, consistent even at partial loads. Indirect evaporative cooling, no mechanical refrigeration. 8.8MW. Potential SOM candidate.
Vantage Dublin DUB1 campus. Annualized PUE 1.2 using virtually no water for cooling. Next-generation energy solution with tidal and wind renewable energy. 16MW.
Green Mountain DC2-Telemark. PUE below 1.15 year-round. Freshwater cooling from nearby lake. 100% Norwegian hydropower. Tier III certified. ~6MW. Potential SOM candidate.
Cologix MTL8 in Montreal. Annualized PUE below 1.3 (LEED Gold certified). ~6MW Scalelogix data center. 99% renewable energy from Hydro-Quebec. Canada's first LEED Gold colocation DC.

facility_name: atNorth SWE04 Solleftea -- 300MW Direct Liquid Cooling Mega Campus (Heat Recovery)
notes: atNorth announced Feb 23, 2026 plans for SWE04, a 300MW mega campus in Solleftea, Sweden. Supports DLC up to 1MW per rack. Chilled water + modular free cooling. Heat reuse integration for circular economy. 50 hectares (Hamre Industrial Park). Path to 200MW power. Cool Nordic climate + 100% renewable energy. Fully zoned and prepared site. Operational H1 2028.

facility_name: SGI-HCM Campus Vietnam -- 200MW AI Data Center (Ho Chi Minh City)
address: Ho Chi Minh City, Ho Chi Minh City
notes: Vietnam's first AI data center: $2.1B SGI-HCM campus breaking ground April 30, 2026 in Cu Chi District, Ho Chi Minh City. 200MW at full buildout, capable of 100,000 GPUs. Developed by AIC (Hong Kong/London) and KinhBac (KBC). Phase 1: 50MW by end 2027. Groundbreaking on Liberation Day (symbolic). Cooling type not yet disclosed, but at 200MW AI scale, liquid cooling highly probable.

facility_name: Submer / Anant Raj Cloud -- Immersion-Cooled Sovereign AI Data Centers (India, Multiple Sites)
notes: Submer (Spain) and Anant Raj Cloud (India) signed MoU Feb 8, 2026 at India AI Impact Summit in New Delhi. Deploying immersion-cooled, modular AI-ready data centers across India. Submer provides full-stack: modular DC design, liquid cooling, prefabricated MEP. Anant Raj provides campuses (Manesar, Panchkula) and operational support. First facilities expected later 2026. EU-India Trade Deal example. Sovereign AI infrastructure for GPU-intensive workloads.

facility_name: CDC Data Centres Brooklyn Campus (BK1) -- 350MW LiquidCore Closed-Loop Cooling (Melbourne AU)
notes: CDC Data Centres formally opened its first Melbourne campus in Brooklyn, Victoria in March 2026. Uses LiquidCore proprietary closed-loop cooling that avoids water consumption for primary cooling. 350MW at full buildout. Paired with 100% renewable energy via Progress Energy programme. Two operational facilities with a third planned.

facility_name: CDC Data Centres Laverton Campus -- 400MW LiquidCore Closed-Loop Cooling (Melbourne AU)
notes: CDC's second Melbourne campus in Laverton North. $2.7B investment. Construction began February 2025. 150MW first phase, 400+MW at full buildout. LiquidCore closed-loop cooling. Combined with Brooklyn campus = 800+MW sovereign capacity for Victoria.

facility_name: Turk Telekom Esenyurt -- Turkiye's First Live Immersion Cooling DC (Istanbul, PUE 1.02-1.06)
notes: Turk Telekom deployed Turkiye's first live liquid immersion cooling system at its Esenyurt data center in Istanbul, January 2026. Domestically developed by Turkish engineers. Achieves PUE 1.02-1.06. 8,700 sqm white space. Tier III facility. Cooling energy use cut by up to 80% vs conventional methods.

facility_name: DataVolt Riyadh First Technology Park -- Two-Phase Immersion + DLC AI Campus (Saudi Arabia)
notes: DataVolt plans a 55,000 sqm AI data center on a MODON plot in eastern Riyadh's First Technology Park. Investment exceeds 2.5B SAR ($660M+ USD). Agreements with Chemours (two-phase DLC + immersion cooling) and Supermicro ($20B rack-scale total liquid cooling). Part of DataVolt's $5B Saudi investment.

facility_name: Khazna Ankara Baskent OIZ -- 100MW AI-Ready Liquid-to-Liquid Cooling (Turkiye)
notes: G42's Khazna Data Centers expanding into Turkiye with a 100MW AI-ready facility at Baskent OIZ near Ankara. Features liquid-to-liquid cooling optimized for AI infrastructure. Low-GWP refrigerants, solar PV, wastewater reuse, HVO-capable generators. First phase cloud-focused; modular design for AI/HPC expansion.

facility_name: Trendyol / Castle Investments Temelli -- 48MW Data Center (Ankara, Turkiye)
notes: Trendyol (Turkey's largest e-commerce platform, Alibaba affiliate) partnered with UAE-based Castle Investments (founded by Gulf Data Hub founder) to build a 48MW data center in Temelli, Ankara. $500M investment. Phase 1 (9.6MW) launching Q3 2026. 40% for Trendyol operations, 60% for cloud/enterprise tenants. Uptime Tier III target.

facility_name: EDGNEX (DAMAC) Cikarang -- 144MW AI-Optimized Data Center (Jakarta, Indonesia)
notes: EDGNEX (DAMAC) investing $2.3B in a 144MW AI-focused data center in Cikarang, Bekasi (east of Jakarta). 50,000 sqm site at Greenland International Industrial Center. PUE target 1.32. Phase 1 operational Dec 2026. One of Southeast Asia's largest AI-dedicated developments.

facility_name: Crusoe Cloud / atNorth ICE02 -- 57MW DLC Expansion (Keflavik, Iceland)
notes: Crusoe Cloud expanded capacity at atNorth's ICE02 data center in Keflavik, Iceland to 57MW (24MW expansion in Aug 2025). Deploying NVIDIA DGX GB200 NVL72 + Blackwell GPUs with Direct Liquid to Chip cooling. 100% renewable (geothermal + hydro). $175M credit facility from Victory Park Capital.

facility_name: Black Sea AI Gigafactory Phase I -- Cernavoda Nuclear-Powered AI Hub (Romania)
notes: Romania bid to host the Black Sea AI Gigafactory under EU InvestAI initiative. 100,000+ AI accelerators across two phases: Phase I at Cernavoda (nuclear power plant site), Phase II at Doicesti (planned SMR co-location). Up to 1,500MW sustainable energy mix (primarily nuclear). ~$5B investment potential. Fiber connectivity + submarine cable links.

facility_name: Yotta Chennai Data Center Park -- 140MW 5-Building AI Campus (India)
notes: Yotta Chennai Data Center Park: 5 interconnected buildings with 25,000 racks and 140MW. Immersion + chilled-water cooling for GPU racks. PUE <1.2 on DLC workloads. Plans to deploy 20,736 liquid-cooled NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GPUs ($2B+ investment) by Aug 2026.

facility_name: Stellium Datacenters Newcastle AI Growth Zone -- 180MW Immersion-Ready HPC Campus (UK)
notes: Stellium's Newcastle campus with Europe's first ORv3 Submer immersion cooling showcase. OCP Ready certified. 4x4MW halls operational, scalable to 80MW (current) and 180MW (full expansion). Designated as UK AI Growth Zone in NE England (Sept 2025). Supports 10-200kW/rack via chilled water + full immersion cooling.

facility_name: Black Sea AI Gigafactory Phase II -- Doicesti SMR-Powered AI Hub (Romania)
notes: Phase II of Romania's Black Sea AI Gigafactory at Doicesti, the site of Romania's planned first SMR (Small Modular Reactor). AI accelerators deployed here after Cernavoda Phase I. SMR co-location offers potential for hybrid cooling and heat exchange. Part of EU InvestAI initiative. Could become EU's first SMR-powered AI facility.
